facts: The patient's name is Wayne.

The patient's blood pressure is 110 over 65.
The patient has a 1 by 2 inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot.
The wound is just proximal to the right fifth metatarsophalangeal (MTP) joint.
The wound has some yellow slough present.
The wound has minimal granulation tissue.
The wound has no surrounding erythema or cellulitis.
The wound has no evidence of fluid collection.
The wound has no necrosis.
The wound has no odor.
The patient has no bony exposure.
The patient has palpable bilateral femoral and popliteal pulses.
The patient has no palpable dorsal pedis (DP) or posterior tibial (PT) pulses.
The patient has Doppler signs present.
The doctor did not appreciate any pain when touching the wound.
The patient's right foot x-ray shows no evidence of osteomyelitis.
The doctor ordered an ankle-brachial index (ABI) to determine the blood supply to the foot.
The doctor performed a debridement to remove dead tissue from the wound.
The doctor prescribed collagenase ointment.
The collagenase ointment is to be applied to the wound once daily.
The wound is to be covered with a dry sterile dressing.
The patient is to wear a surgical shoe to take pressure off the area.
The doctor does not think more antibiotics are needed at this time.
The patient is to follow up in two weeks.
